title: phacelia californica var. calycosa
intro: Phacelia californica var. calycosa, also known as California phacelia or wild heliotrope, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and taproot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phacelia californica var. calycosa include its potential to aid in the treatment of colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an expectorant to help relieve bronchitis and coughs. Phacelia californica var. calycosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also sometimes used topically to soothe skin conditions and promote wound healing.
